subject: [HOLMES] Changed the way for MSB registration
commit_message:
[HOLMES] Changed the way for MSB registration

Register Holmes MSB through an annotation in the Helm chart.
Enable/Disable TLS via an env variable.
Added Ingress Configurations

Issue-ID: HOLMES-612
Signed-off-by: Guangrong Fu <fu.guangrong@zte.com.cn>
Change-Id: If1bd8a62ea3840943ccd04cbcc16442f65fd434b

Patch Set 3: -Code-Review

(1 comment)

I've rebased our codes. Let's see if things are getting OK.
Btw, I added MSB registration in the "annotations" section as Andreas suggested. In my opinion, MSB has not been announced deprecated officially by ONAP, so it's better for us to mark it as an alternative that's controlled by the switch ".Values.global.msbEnabled".

The last gating run has some issues with smoke tests but may not be related.

Patch Set 3:

Hi,
the MSB is still an option and the annotation is fine.
But I see in the logs of rule-management pod some SSL related exceptions.
Could you check, whether this can be fixed by configuration or if there is a fix in the pod required:

https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//133666-3/k8s-teardown/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-holmes-rule-mgmt-58d74f448-lcjgj-holmes-rule-mgmt-logs.html

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 3 on the queue.

Patch Set 4:

Hi,
I found the missing configuration and fixed it.
Now the only problem is, that still the rule-mgmt pod tries to actively register to MSB.
This would need a fix in the image.
But it gives it up after a few retries.
Best regards
Andreas

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

(6 comments)

Hi Guangrong,
I think the recent changes are not needed (see my comments)
All ingress related options are handled by the _ingress.tpl.
As I see in the Architecture diagram (https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/HOLMES+Component+Documentations+Kohn-R11)
only rule-management exposes it's ports to an external client, so no Ingress definition is required for "engine-mgmt", IMHO.
Best regards
Andreas

Patch Set 9:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> Hi,
> I found the missing configuration and fixed it.
> Now the only problem is, that still the rule-mgmt pod tries to actively register to MSB.
> This would need a fix in the image.
> But it gives it up after a few retries.
> Best regards
> Andreas

Andreas, we decided the leave this part unchaged as MSB is still optional in R12. The logs you found will cause no side effect (unless you consider the log information itself a problem). Once the TSC decide that MSB should be totally removed, we'll remove the corresponding codes.
